---
title: "Significant Tree Inventory for Newark Parks and Recreation (December 2025)"
subtitle: "An Inventory of Tree Resources in Phillips, Lewis, and Kells Park and Along the James F. Hall Trail Corridor"
author:
  - name: Winnie Li
  - name: Ikechukwu Ogbonnia
  - name: Matthew Saponaro
    degrees: PhD
prepared-for:
  - name: City of Newark Parks & Recreation
    location: Newark, Delaware
affiliation:
  - name: Technology Service Center, University of Delaware
    url: https://www.sites.udel.edu/technology-service-center
date: January 20, 2026
version: "1.0"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
execute:
  echo: false
---

**Prepared by**  
Technology Service Center, University of Delaware  

**Prepared for**  
City of Newark Parks & Recreation  
Newark, Delaware

## Contact Information
For questions regarding this report, please contact Matthew Saponaro, Ph.D., Director of the [Technology Service Center](https://www.sites.udel.edu/technology-service-center) at University of Delaware

## Motivation

The purpose of this inventory is to identify, measure, and document significant trees across the [James F. Hall Trail](https://delawaregreenways.org/trail/james-f-hall-trail/) and three local parks (Phillips, Lewis, and Kells). The collected data will support planning, maintenance, ecological assessments, and long-term urban forestry initiatives.

## Overview

In total, 158 significant trees were inventoried. Of these, 114 are large mature trees (72.15%), 8 are young trees (5.06%), and 36 are newly planted (22.78%). See figure [6](inventoryInsights.html#tree-type-distribution-across-sites) for more details. In this project, “significant trees” were defined as follows:

-   **Large mature trees**: Any trees with a Diameter at Breast height (DBH) ≥ 20 inches
-   **Young tree**: Young, intentionally planted trees that have reached a stage of growth where supplemental support is no longer present
-   **Newly planted trees**: Young, intentionally planted trees with supplemental support (e.g., wooden stakes or protective cages)

## Inventoried Locations

We collected the data on James F. Hall Trail, a widely used trail that runs through three local parks: Phillips, Lewis, and Kells. The inventory included significant trees located within a 15-foot buffer of the paved trail or within a 15-foot fall distance of park amenities. To maintain an organized approach, the trail was divided into 8 smaller sections (JH-W, JH-P, JH-A, JH-L, JH-K, JH-R, JH-UD, and JH-E). Including the three individual park sites (PHIL, LEW, KELL), the study encompassed a total of 11 inventory locations.

<iframe src="https://www.google.com/maps/d/embed?mid=1L1EOKHYYIpvG8KEbe4iMEppFlZ598jA&amp;ehbc=2E312F" width="640" height="480" style="border:0; display: block; margin: 0 auto;" loading="lazy" referrerpolicy="no-referrer-when-downgrade">

</iframe>

<br/> **Figure 1.** Map showing inventoried tree locations along the James F. Hall Trail and surrounding parks.
